metadata.name: sigmoid_threshold_2016
metadata.description:
Finds the economic_split threshold for v27 victory in the 2016-block retarget regime WITH the sigmoid price oracle enabled.
MOTIVATION: sigmoid_2016_retarget showed that sigmoid fires the pool cascade ~4,185s earlier (t≈4,241s vs t≈8,426s baseline) and cuts v26 block count by ~48% (799 vs 1,543). Critically, sigmoid tested econ=[0.35, 0.50, 0.60, 0.70] and ALL gave v27_dominant. The baseline threshold is bracketed at (0.50, 0.55) — but under sigmoid econ=0.35 is STILL v27_dominant. So the sigmoid threshold is somewhere below 0.35.
Research question: What is the minimum economic_split for v27 to win under the sigmoid oracle + 2016-block retarget regime? Does v26 ever win?
PRICE ORACLE MATH (sigmoid starting gap at 50/50 hashrate):
  econ=0.20 → v26 premium ~12.8% — neutral pools strongly prefer v26 from t=0
  econ=0.25 → v26 premium ~8.0%  — moderate v26 preference
  econ=0.30 → v26 premium ~3.6%  — weak v26 preference; sigmoid may amplify this

The committed v27 pool tolerance is 0.51 × 0.26 = 13.26%. The sigmoid amplifies price gaps relative to baseline — so a 12.8% initial gap under baseline may push above 13.26% under sigmoid, potentially enabling a v26 cascade even before retarget.
EXPECTED OUTCOMES:
  econ=0.20: v26 cascade possible; if price gap under sigmoid > 13.26% → v26_dominant
  econ=0.25: borderline; likely v27_dominant but later/closer cascade
  econ=0.30: v27_dominant; similar to econ=0.35 baseline

PRIOR RESULTS FOR CONTEXT:
  econ=0.20 (144-block, baseline): v26_dominant (cascade at t=624s, 1846 v26 blocks)
  econ=0.35 (2016-block, sigmoid): v27_dominant (cascade at t=4,241s, 799 v26 blocks)
  econ=0.50 (2016-block, baseline): v26_dominant (197 v27 blocks — sweep_11)

Run across TWO namespaces for parallelism:

  Namespace A — scenarios 0-1 (econ=0.20, 0.25):
    python 3_run_sweep.py \
      --input sigmoid_threshold_2016/build_manifest.json \
      --namespace sweep-sig-thresh-a \
      --results-dir sigmoid_threshold_2016/results \
      --duration 9000 --retarget-interval 2016 --interval 2 \
      --use-sigmoid \
      --scenarios sweep_0000 sweep_0001

  Namespace B — scenario 2 (econ=0.30):
    python 3_run_sweep.py \
      --input sigmoid_threshold_2016/build_manifest.json \
      --namespace sweep-sig-thresh-b \
      --results-dir sigmoid_threshold_2016/results \
      --duration 9000 --retarget-interval 2016 --interval 2 \
      --use-sigmoid \
      --scenarios sweep_0002

Namespaces to create first:
  kubectl create namespace sweep-sig-thresh-a
  kubectl create namespace sweep-sig-thresh-b

Wall-clock estimate:
  Namespace A: 2 scenarios × 9,000s = ~5h
  Namespace B: 1 scenario  × 9,000s = ~2.5h
  Overall bottleneck: ~5h

NOTE: Both namespaces write to the same results dir (different scenario subdirs).
